description:
Initial Setup (Steps 0-10)

  1. Install SWAT on default host for A_DL0_STD_1

  2. Verify microcode level is greater than REDWOOD

  3. Disable DCB (Dynamic Cache Balancing) - inline: 80,DCB,CRTL,DSBL

  4. Set early exit from active cache conversion - inline: 65,CSEG,PLCY,EXIT,SET

  5. Add A1 counter on A_DL0_STD_1

  6. Get cache configuration - inline: E7,CF

  7. Parse and store number of configured engines (numOfengines variable)

  8. Get baseline errors from all directors

  9. Get cache configuration again - inline: E7,CF

  10. Parse system_type variable from output

  11. Parse engine_count variable from output

Launch Parallel Compression/Dedup Workloads (Steps 11-14)

  1. Launch workload on A_parallel1_STD_1: CR 4:1, Dedup 1.5:1, 10% read, random I/O (continuous)

  2. Launch workload on A_parallel2_STD_1: CR 8:1, Dedup 2:1, 20% read, random I/O (continuous)

  3. Launch workload on A_parallel3_STD_1: CR 16:1, Dedup 3:1, 30% read, random I/O (continuous)

  4. Wait 30 minutes

Round 1: EM and DN Director Drops (Steps 15-53)

  1. Pick random online EM director

  2. Pick random online DN director (different board than EM)

  3. Wait 30 seconds

  4. Drop EM director (set to DD - Dead Director)

  5. Drop EM director again (second EM)

  6. Wait 15 minutes

  7. Drop DN director (set to DD)

  8. Wait 15 minutes

  9. Create 20 thin devices (262668 CYL, FBA emulation, label: new_devs)

  10. Pick existing masking storage group

  11. Add new_devs to storage group

  12. Wait 30 seconds

  13. Update device paths for new_devs

  14. Wait 1 minute

  15. Launch I/O on new_devs: 50% write, 50% read, random CR/Dedup ratios (continuous)

  16. Wait 30 minutes

  17. Stop I/O on new_devs instantly

  18. Poll for no write pending on new_devs

  19. Wait 5 minutes

  20. Remove new_devs from storage group

  21. Wait 10 seconds

  22. Unmap new_devs and refresh host

  23. Wait 10 seconds

  24. Delete new_devs (with deallocation)

  25. Wait 10 seconds

  26. Refresh host

  27. Recover first EM director from DD

  28. Wait 5 minutes

  29. Recover second EM director from DD

  30. Wait 5 minutes

  31. Recover DN director from DD

  32. Wait 15 minutes

  33. Get errors from all directors

  34. Get differential errors (compare baseline to current)

  35. Define custom RUL file for EM/DN drop (skip expected errors)

  36. Analyze errors using custom RUL file

  37. Define alternate custom RUL file for EM/DN drop

  38. Analyze errors using alternate RUL file

  39. Wait 15 minutes

Round 2: EM and OR Director Drops (Steps 54-94)

  1. Pick random online EM director

  2. Wait 30 seconds

  3. Pick random online OR director (different board than EM)

  4. Wait 30 seconds

  5. Drop EM director (set to DD)

  6. Wait 15 minutes

  7. Create 20 thin devices (262668 CYL, FBA emulation, label: new_devs2)

  8. Add new_devs2 to storage group

  9. Wait 30 seconds

  10. Update device paths for new_devs2

  11. Wait 1 minute

  12. Drop OR director (set to DD)

  13. Wait 15 minutes

  14. Drop second OR director (for single engine scenario)

  15. Wait 15 minutes

  16. Launch I/O on new_devs2: 70% write, 30% read, random CR/Dedup ratios (continuous)

  17. Wait 30 minutes

  18. Stop I/O on new_devs2 instantly

  19. Poll for no write pending on new_devs2

  20. Wait 5 minutes

  21. Remove new_devs2 from storage group

  22. Wait 10 seconds

  23. Unmap new_devs2 and refresh host

  24. Wait 10 seconds

  25. Delete new_devs2 (with deallocation)

  26. Wait 10 seconds

  27. Refresh host

  28. Recover EM director from DD

  29. Wait 5 minutes

  30. Recover first OR director from DD

  31. Wait 2 minutes

  32. Set recovered OR director online

  33. Recover second OR director from DD

  34. Wait 2 minutes

  35. Set recovered OR director online

  36. Get errors from all directors

  37. Wait 15 minutes

  38. Get differential errors (compare to previous checkpoint)

  39. Define custom RUL file for EM/OR drop

  40. Analyze errors using custom RUL file

  41. Wait 15 minutes

Round 3: DN and OR Director Drops (Steps 95-136)

  1. Pick random online DN director

  2. Wait 30 seconds

  3. Pick random online OR director (different board than DN)

  4. Wait 30 seconds

  5. Create 20 thin devices (262668 CYL, FBA emulation, label: new_devs3)

  6. Add new_devs3 to storage group

  7. Wait 30 seconds

  8. Update device paths for new_devs3

  9. Wait 1 minute

  10. Drop DN director (single engine scenario only)

  11. Wait 15 minutes

  12. Drop second DN director (set to DD)

  13. Wait 15 minutes

  14. Drop OR director (set to DD)

  15. Wait 15 minutes

  16. Launch I/O on new_devs3: 60% write, 40% read, random CR/Dedup ratios (continuous)

  17. Wait 30 minutes

  18. Stop I/O on new_devs3 instantly

  19. Poll for no write pending on new_devs3

  20. Wait 5 minutes

  21. Remove new_devs3 from storage group

  22. Wait 10 seconds

  23. Unmap new_devs3 and refresh host

  24. Wait 10 seconds

  25. Delete new_devs3 (with deallocation)

  26. Wait 10 seconds

  27. Refresh host

  28. Recover first DN director from DD

  29. Wait 5 minutes

  30. Recover second DN director from DD

  31. Wait 5 minutes

  32. Recover OR director from DD

  33. Wait 1 minute

  34. Set recovered OR director online

  35. Wait 15 minutes

  36. Get errors from all directors

  37. Get differential errors (compare to previous checkpoint)

  38. Define custom RUL file for DN/OR drop

  39. Analyze errors using custom RUL file

  40. Define alternate custom RUL file for DN/OR drop

  41. Analyze errors using alternate RUL file

  42. Wait 15 minutes

Cleanup and Final Validation (Steps 137-169)

  1. Stop parallel I/O workload on A_parallel1_STD_1

  2. Stop parallel I/O workload on A_parallel2_STD_1

  3. Stop parallel I/O workload on A_parallel3_STD_1

  4. Wait 30 minutes

  5. Get errors from all EM directors

  6. Check DWCM archive records - inline: 65,DWCM,AREC

  7. Poll for error 30.6A3E.4C (3s interval, 10 iterations, expect 1 occurrence)

  8. Poll for error 30.6A3E.48 (3s interval, 10 iterations, expect 1 occurrence)

  9. Poll for no write pending on A_parallel1_STD_1

  10. Poll for no write pending on A_parallel2_STD_1

  11. Poll for no write pending on A_parallel3_STD_1

  12. Add A1 counter

  13. Wait 2 minutes

  14. Enable DCB - inline: 80,DCB,CRTL,ENBL

  15. Stop I/O on A_parallel1_STD_1 (redundant check)

  16. Stop I/O on A_parallel2_STD_1 (redundant check)

  17. Stop I/O on A_parallel3_STD_1 (redundant check)

  18. Poll for no write pending on A_parallel1_STD_1

  19. Poll for no write pending on A_parallel2_STD_1

  20. Poll for no write pending on A_parallel3_STD_1

  21. Recover all DD directors

  22. Wait 1 minute

  23. Set all directors online

  24. Wait 1 minute

  25. Verify all directors are ONLINE

  26. Enable DCB - inline: 80,DCB,CTRL,ENBL

  27. Get baseline errors for CBNK scan

  28. Start CBNK (Cache Bank) scan on A_CBNK_STD_1 (TDEV devices)

  29. Poll for CBNK scan completion (3 hour timeout, 30s interval)

  30. Get errors after CBNK scan

  31. Get differential errors for CBNK scan

  32. Define custom RUL file for CBNK scan

  33. Analyze CBNK scan errors using custom RUL file

Key Validation Points:

  • System remains operational during multiple director failures across three rounds

  • Compression/deduplication I/O continues during director drops

  • Device lifecycle operations (create/map/I/O/unmap/delete) succeed during degraded state

  • All directors successfully recover from DD state

  • Error analysis confirms only expected errors occurred

  • Cache bank scan completes successfully after all recoveries

  • DCB functionality restored at test completion

Test Coverage:

  • Round 1: EM + DN director failures

  • Round 2: EM + OR director failures

  • Round 3: DN + OR director failures

  • Validates resilience across all critical director types with active compression/dedup workloads
